meta.title: The Explorer Mindset - A Guide to Growth for Your Life, Family, and Work
meta.description: By Jean-Philippe Gauthier. A practical guide to building curiosity, adaptability, and resilience for navigating an uncertain future. Foreword by Sean Downey.

hero.subtitle: A Guide to Growth for Your Life, Family, and Work
hero.author: Jean-Philippe Gauthier

aboutBook.pullQuote: “Swap safety for curiosity and discover how energizing that choice can be.”
aboutBook.description1: Change isn’t slowing down. It shapes how we lead, parent, and live. The question isn’t whether you can keep up. It’s how you’ll move through it.
aboutBook.description2: This guide shows why an Explorer Mindset fuels happiness, resilience, and forward momentum. You’ll find practical ways to experiment every day, adapt on the fly, transform setbacks into springboards, and ask bigger questions that pull you toward purpose.

buyBook.heading: Start your journey today.
buyBook.subtitle: Available in three formats. Choose the one that fits your adventure.

digital.description: Instant access on Kindle. Read anywhere, anytime.

hardcover.description: Premium edition. A beautiful addition to your bookshelf.

softcover.name: Softcover
softcover.description: Lightweight and portable. Perfect for reading on the go.

pillars.title: Curiosity
pillars.description: The desire to understand and learn. To ask “what if” before settling for “that’s how it is.”

pillars.title: Adaptability
pillars.description: The ability to adjust when the ground shifts. To welcome detours as part of the journey.

pillars.title: Resilience
pillars.description: The capacity to recover and keep moving. To turn every fall into forward momentum.

aboutAuthor.name: Jean-Philippe Gauthier
aboutAuthor.bio1: Jean-Philippe (JP) Gauthier is a father, a coach, and a work in progress. Over a thirty-year career, including more than a decade at Google, he led teams through constant change and learned, often the hard way, what it actually takes to stay curious, adaptable, and resilient when life doesn’t go as planned.
aboutAuthor.bio2: He’ll be the first to tell you he’s no natural-born risk-taker. Just an ordinary man who developed this mindset to navigate life’s inevitable twists.
aboutAuthor.bio3: He wrote this book out of a deep desire to guide his children, Anaïs and Louis, and his nephews, Jules and Samuel. The fact that you’re holding it is a happy accident he’s grateful for.

assessment.heading: The Explorer Mindset Assessment
assessment.instructions: For each statement, rate yourself honestly from 1 to 5.

sections.title: Curiosity
questions: When I encounter something I don’t understand, my first instinct is to explore it rather than move past it.
I actively seek out experiences that challenge how I currently see the world.
I find myself genuinely interested in people whose lives and perspectives are very different from mine.
Uncertainty about something makes me want to learn more, not less.
I regularly make time to follow ideas or questions that have no immediate practical use, just because they interest me.
When a conversation takes an unexpected turn, I lean in rather than steer back to familiar ground.

sections.title: Adaptability
questions: When my plans fall apart, I look for a new path rather than dwelling on what didn’t work.
I can see multiple ways to approach a problem, even when I’m under pressure.
I’m comfortable changing my mind when new information or a better perspective comes along.
I adjust my communication style depending on who I’m talking to and what the situation calls for.
When something in my life shifts unexpectedly, I find ways to make it work rather than waiting for things to go back to normal.
I treat new challenges as opportunities to learn something, even when they’re unwelcome.

sections.title: Resilience
questions: When something knocks me down, I find my footing again without staying down too long.
I tend to come through difficult periods feeling like I’ve grown from them.
I don’t need everything to be perfect or certain before I can move forward.
During hard times, I hold onto the belief that things will improve.
I can manage stress without it derailing the things that matter most to me.
After a setback, I’m able to reflect on what happened without getting stuck in self-criticism.

gate.heading: You’re almost there.
gate.description: Enter your details to reveal your Explorer Profile.
gate.privacyNote: No spam. We respect your privacy.

results.footnote: This tool is designed for reflection, not diagnosis. Inspired by peer-reviewed research including Kashdan et al. (2009), Dennis & Vander Wal (2010), and Smith et al. (2008).

mapmaker.name: The Mapmaker
mapmaker.description: You’re at the beginning of the journey, and that’s a perfectly honest place to be. The Explorer Mindset isn’t something you’re born with; it’s something you build. Start with one trait and go from there.

pathfinder.name: The Pathfinder
pathfinder.description: You have real strengths to build on. Some habits are forming; others are still inconsistent. That’s normal. Pick one area from your lowest section and try one small thing differently this week.

trailblazer.name: The Trailblazer
trailblazer.description: You’re living much of this already. The next frontier isn’t just personal growth. It’s helping others find their own path. Who in your life could benefit from what you already know?

pioneer.name: The Pioneer
pioneer.description: You’ve internalized the Explorer Mindset in a meaningful way. These aren’t just concepts. They’re habits. Stay humble, stay curious. What’s the next edge you want to push?

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.whatWeCollectText: When you subscribe to The Explorer Mindset newsletter, we collect only your email address. We do not collect names, payment information, browsing history, or any other personal data.
privacy.howWeUseText: Your email address is used solely to send you occasional reflections on curiosity, adaptability, and resilience related to the book. We will never sell, rent, or share your email address with third parties.
privacy.whereStoredText: Your email is stored securely in our database hosted by Convex, which uses encryption at rest and in transit. The website is hosted on Vercel.
privacy.rightUnsubscribe: Unsubscribe at any time using the link in our emails or by contacting us
privacy.rightDeletion: Request deletion of your data by contacting us
privacy.rightKnow: Know what data we hold about you (it’s only your email)
privacy.cookiesText: This website does not use cookies and does not run any analytics or tracking scripts.
privacy.retentionText: We retain your email address for as long as you remain subscribed. Once you unsubscribe, your record is marked as inactive. You may request full deletion at any time.
privacy.contactText: For any privacy-related questions, please reach out at theexplorermindset.com.
